manual_verification_required.risk: Real off-thread execution and cross-mount cleanup correctness cannot be observed by any test in this jsdom-less, no-Worker-mocking repo (architecture.md Implementation Constraints).
manual_verification_required.detail: The hook's `cancelled` flag (set on effect cleanup, checked inside `applyResult`) is meant to stop a stale Worker message, a stale `worker.onerror` firing, or a stale scheduled `requestIdleCallback`/`setTimeout` fallback callback from calling `setGeneratedSvg` after the effect has re-run (theme change) or the component has unmounted, and `worker?.terminate()` is meant to avoid leaking a live Worker thread across re-renders. Both are structurally present and route every async result through the single `applyResult` helper (verified by source read, not by a mounted-component test), but no jsdom/RTL exists in this repo to actually mount/re-render/unmount the hook and observe the guard firing.
manual_verification_required.recommended_check: In a real browser (or once Phase B's aquarelle theme exists, S8), rapidly toggle between a dynamic-banner theme and a different theme (or navigate away) while the banner is mid-generation, and confirm via DevTools (Application > Frameworks or a console log) that no stale SVG from the abandoned attempt is ever applied, and that no orphaned Worker threads accumulate across repeated toggles.

manual_verification_required.risk: Worker bundling under `output: 'export'` is the story's own acknowledged risk (architecture.md Boundary Rule 11) — verified via `make build` in this session, but that is a single build-time observation, not a standing regression test.
manual_verification_required.detail: `make build` was run and succeeded. The emitted `_app` chunk contains `new Worker(n.tu(new URL(n.p+n.u(6865),n.b)))`, and `out/_next/static/chunks/6865.e5beb5c9dc6fac5a.js` is a genuinely separate chunk containing the worker's `self.addEventListener` and the `watercolor` generator body (confirmed independently in this review pass: `grep -c 'self.addEventListener' 6865.<hash>.js` = 1; the same string count in the main `_app` chunk = 0, so the worker's runtime side effect is not leaking into the main-thread bundle via the type-only import). No `next.config.mjs` change was needed or made.
manual_verification_required.recommended_check: Re-confirm `make build` stays green on CI/future Next.js version bumps, since webpack's native `new Worker(new URL(...))` recognition is a build-time feature, not something a unit test can regression-guard. A real-browser load of a dynamic-banner theme (once one exists, Phase B) would additionally confirm the worker actually executes off-thread rather than merely bundling correctly.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: Nothing harder than expected in the implementation itself; the one friction point was a false-positive in the AC-PERF-2 structural regex scanner (it matched the literal word randomizeParams inside a header-comment sentence, not code) that needed a one-line comment reword to clear — see surprised_by for the positive surprise (worker bundling risk did not materialize).
retrospective.surprised_by: The story's own acknowledged risk (architecture.md Boundary Rule 11: worker bundling under `output: 'export'` might prove fussy and need the `requestIdleCallback` escape hatch) did not materialize — `make build` succeeded on the first attempt with Next.js 14's native webpack5 `new Worker(new URL(...))` pattern, no `next.config.mjs` change needed. Verified concretely, not just by absence of a build error: the worker's `self.addEventListener` appears in an isolated chunk and zero times in the main `_app` chunk.

absorbed_from.note: Dev's own retro was skipped (clean run; one minor iteration where the worker file's header-comment prose contained the literal word 'randomizeParams', which false-tripped the AC-PERF-2 structural regex scanner in useDynamicBanner.test.ts — reworded the comment, no test or contract change needed). Dev also independently confirmed the make-build worker-chunk evidence I re-verified above.
